page: Amazon Web Services m8idb.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, general-purpose server instance powered by Intel x86_64 processors. It features 192 virtual CPUs across 96 physical cores with a dedicated allocation and a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of system memory, maintaining a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per physical core.

This server configuration includes 11400 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage, providing high-capacity, low-latency local data access. The network interface supports a baseline bandwidth of 200 Gbps, facilitating high-throughput data transfers and low-latency communication for distributed environments. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included with this instance.

The bare-metal architecture of the m8idb.metal-48xl eliminates virtualization overhead, offering direct access to the underlying Intel hardware. This resource density and high-capacity local storage make it qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring massive local scratch space, high-throughput network performance, and significant parallel processing capabilities. Suitable workloads include large-scale databases, data warehousing, enterprise applications, and high-throughput data processing pipelines.
description: Amazon Web Services m8idb.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, general-purpose server powered by Intel x86_64 processors running at 3.9 GHz. It features 192 vCPUs (96 physical cores) and 768.0 GB of system memory, establishing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. For storage-heavy operations, the instance includes 11400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, paired with a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps. The bare-metal configuration provides direct hardware access, eliminating virtualization overhead to maximize resource efficiency. This server is designed for workloads requiring high-throughput local storage, substantial memory capacity, and high network performance, such as large-scale databases, enterprise applications, and data processing pipelines.
